question_text: A smartphone battery stores 14,400 joules of energy. If the phone uses power at a rate of 3 watts while idle, approximately how many hours could the phone last on a full charge?
explanation: Using P = E ÷ t, we can rearrange to find t = E ÷ P. Time = 14,400 ÷ 3 = 4800 seconds. Convert to hours: 4800 ÷ 3600 = 1.33 hours (approximately). In reality, phone batteries last longer because phones use less power when idle than when actively in use.
